description_long: This is an electric guitar with a bold, angular body shape reminiscent of the Gibson Explorer, featuring a striking blue finish on the front face and a black border outlining the body. The guitar has a long, straight neck with a dark fretboard and visible frets, leading to a headstock with six tuning pegs arranged in a single row. The body includes two visible pickups and a set of control knobs, likely for volume and tone adjustment, as well as a bridge and tailpiece typical of electric guitars. The back of the guitar is solid black, with no visible electronics or cavity covers. The side views reveal the instrument's slim profile and the slight protrusion of the control knobs and bridge hardware. This guitar's design and color scheme make it visually striking and suitable for stage performance or urban street music scenes.
description: A blue and black angular electric guitar with a long neck, six tuning pegs, and visible pickups and control knobs. Its bold, modern design stands out with a sharp, geometric body shape.
description_view_0: Front view showing the blue face, black border, pickups, control knobs, and full neck with tuning pegs.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the guitar's slim profile and the slight protrusion of hardware components.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a solid black back with no visible electronics or covers.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again highlighting the thinness and hardware protrusions.
attribute_headstock_design: Angled headstock with six inline tuning pegs, no visible logo, and a simple, pointed contour.
